title: New Balance Men's 877 V1 Walking Shoe
description: The New Balance 877 walking shoe is designed to help you move in comfort. Featuring the same sole as the iconic 577 model from New Balance, this stylish walking shoe for adults delivers premium comfort and support with a fresh new look. Equipped with Walking Strike Path technology in the outsole, these cushioned shoes promote a proper stride by helping to stabilize and guide your feet trough their natural walking gait cycle. Other technological features include ABZORB midsole technology that combines cushioning and compression resistance to help minimize the damage from impacts as you log your miles. A durable rubber outsole stands up well to everyday wear-and-tear so you can keep moving with confidence. While the 877 does a great job of protecting and supporting your feet, it’s no slouch in the style department either. These comfortable walking shoes have a sleek athletic look inspired by New Balance heritage designs. The suede and mesh upper pops with classic New Balance branding and reflective details that are designed to catch the light – and the attention of your walking buddies. These shoes were proudly assembled in New Balance's U.S. factories – since the domestic value is less than 70%, we have labeled them "Assembled in the USA." Select styles of the 877 may be eligible for Medicare reimbursement with Medicare/HCPCS code A5500. 100% Suede/mesh Imported Rubber sole 11 mm drop: due to variances created during the development and manufacturing processes, all references to 11 mm drop are approximate Medicare / HCPCS code = A5500, may be eligible for Medicare reimbursement Rubber outsole Suede/mesh upper These shoes were proudly assembled in our U.S. factories by our more than 1,200 U.S. workers. title: New Balance Kids' 680 V6 Hook and Loop Running Shoe
description: The New Balance 680 v6 kids' running shoe is a sneaker that blends style and performance to satisfy both kids and adults alike. A modern design ups your little one's cool factor, featuring a no-sew construction for a sleek fit and feel that's snug and secure without being too tight, delivering both a comfortable fit and contemporary style. The streamlined silhouette on these running shoes for kids includes leather and mesh upper material with a stitched-down tip and foxing details for flexible support and lasting durability. These kids' performance shoes also include an ABZORB midsole that absorbs impact through a combination of cushioning and compression resistance, for a cushioned ride that takes kiddos from the classroom to the playground and everywhere in between. These comfortable kids' running shoes are available in a variety of colors that range from neutral to bold and bright, so your child can pick the one that suits their style and boosts their confidence. Rubber sole Power in Simplicity: The New Balance 680v6 running shoe for kids features a minimal leather and open mesh upper, offering flexible support and easy-breezy comfort Modern Construction for an Outstanding Fit: The leather/mesh upper on these New Balance kids' running shoes features a stitched-down tip and foxing details to provide flexible support and durability Soft Underfoot Cushioning: The ABZORB midsole absorbs impact through a combination of cushioning and compression resistance, giving these kids' running shoes a comfortable feel that lasts all day Designed for Support and Durability: These kids' performance shoes feature a strong rubber outsole to offer long-lasting durability, built to stand up to daily use and playground wear-and-tear Available in a Range of Colors: From neutral tones to bold hues, these cushioned running shoes for kids are available in a range of shades to suit the style and color preferences of your little one

title: Brooks Men's Adrenaline GTS 22 Supportive Running Shoe
description: Made in the USA or Imported Rubber sole THIS MEN'S SHOE IS FOR: Runners looking for a smooth ride, with great cushion and trusted GuideRails support that won’t distract from the fun of the run. This Brooks Adrenaline GTS 22 is a certified PDAC A5500 Diabetic shoe and has been granted the APMA Seal of Acceptance. We recommend ordering Brooks running shoes 1/2 to one size larger than your dress shoe. SUPPORT AND CUSHION: Provides just the right amount of stability and support, great for overpronation while providing high energizing cushioning. Ideal for road running, cross training, the gym or wherever you might want to take them! Predecessor: Adrenaline GTS 21 BALANCED, SOFT CUSHIONING: The updated midsole is now 100% DNA LOFT cushioning paired with the Segmented Crash Pad for an even softer, smoother ride and easier flow from landing to toe-off. MODERNIZED FIT: More strategic use of the 3D Fit Print upper technology offers the structure and proven fit the Adrenaline is known for without excess bulk. GUIDERAILS HOLISTIC SUPPORT SYSTEM: We've shifted our focus beyond the feet to the most injury-prone part of a runner’s body: the knees. GuideRails keep you moving comfortably by keeping excess movement in check.

reviews.title: Perfect runners shoe for marathons and races
reviews.text: I run 5K races up and down the eastern US and have used Brooks brand shoes for over 25 years now. I absolutely love their brand their style and the absolute comfort of their brand. The adrenaline line and ghost line of shoes and perfect for cross training, running, and general comfort wear. I am a long term lover of Brooks shoes and no other company matches their level of quality and comfort.
reviews.author: AHTQAFB2ATO2ITSWZDJQFPAFF74Q

reviews.title: Great shoes in the early going!
reviews.text: I read a few reviews of these, and a lot of folks said they were tight and had a long break-in period.  I just wanted to get a review up because my experience has been the opposite!  So far, I've put about 35 miles on them.  I did some cross training work outs in them (mainly boxing) just to break them in for a couple hours before running in them.  So far, so good!  I'm a 10.5 wide, and I have actually found the toe box to fit the same as my 21s.  They also don't ride up as high on my ankle in the back as the 21s, so I've avoided the nasty blisters the 21s would sometimes give me.  All in all, I'm loving these so far!  They're a good training shoe.  Supportive, maybe a little heavy but I can live with that for training.  So far so good!
reviews.author: AEOGQWPSPNENTXCTJTVI6GAYOFZQ

reviews.title: Overall, comfortable
reviews.text: These are odd shoes, the shape and the colors clash. There's no stitching or closure to the edges on the tongue so it's just bare foam which looks cheap. The top part has a bunch of different textures and fabrics, plastic, leather, cloth, suede, foam, very thin jacket like material. I don't like that it looks like it's pulling up by the time box, like it will separate. If you normally wear no show socks you might want to use a fuller coverage pair with these. I had some uncomfortable rubbing at my ankle and the top of my foot from the weird seams inside. The shoe is comfortable to stand and walk in and the sole is pretty grippy.

reviews.title: Good comfortable everyday shoes
reviews.text: Adidas memory foam sneakers are my absolute favorite for everyday wear. I doubt these would be supportive enough for running, but they’re great for walking and day-to-day use. I walked probably 10+ miles over two days wearing these shoes right out of the box with no break-in, and they felt great. My only complaint is that they don’t seem to last as long as some other sneakers - for me, the tread wears down considerably and the white soles get yucky within just a few months. But, for the price, I think they’re worth it

title: Etnies Mens Kingpin Skate Skate Sneakers Casual Shoes Casual - Black
description: Borrowing a little design inspiration from the etnies Fader, the kingpin is a classic puffy tongue skateboard shoe Rip it up like skater royalty in the Kingpin shoe from etnies. The sturdy upper is perforated for breathability and offers adjustable lacing, while heavy-duty padding on the collar and tongue adds the stability you need to land your most difficult tricks. The herringbone-treaded outsole offers superior grip, whether you need it for the ramps or the street. Established in 1986, etnies is the first skateboarder-owned and skateboarder-operated global action sports footwear and apparel company. etnies not only pushed the envelope by creating the first pro model skate shoe, but it pioneered technological advances and changed the face of skateboard footwear forever. Today, etnies' vision is to remain the leading action sports company committed to creating functional products that provide the most style, comfort, durability and protection possible. etnies stays true to its roots by sponsoring a world-class team of skateboarding, surfing, snowboarding, moto-x, and BMX athletes and continues its dedication by giving back to each of these communities. I looked all thought the Amazon web pages but as I was looking for pictures and descriptions of the soles I had a difficult time because the web pages do not show the soles.  Then went to a high profile name brand brick and mortar store but this store didn't have what I wanted anyway so I went back to Amazon and purchased this pair of Reeboks.  On this particular web page there was just enough of the tip of the sole showing to give me a good idea of the sole and as it was acceptable to me, I then made my purchase and it appears to be very satisfactory.

reviews.text:

I have had issues with NB not bending at the right place for my foot. That has caused toe discomfort and even cramping. These do not to this. It may be my foot, but I'm a big Reebok fan because of it.

Polish up nicely too so you can impress the coaches - LOL!

I believe they can also be worn as dress casual with black slacks to do presentation as well.

Just a good option for football and baseball officials!

reviews.title: Saucony, what is going on?
reviews.text:
I used to LOVE Saucony shoes. Now I can't find one that fits. I've been a size 8.5 for decades, and Saucony 8.5 used to fit. I still have a pair of Saucony Stabil shoes that fit great (Too bad they don't make those anymore!)
Now I try size 8.5 and my toes are crammed to the end of the toebox. This is the second time this has happened, with two different styles. Maybe the next size up would fit, but who wants to ship back and forth looking for an appropriate replacement. Maybe Saucony needs to re-size their shoes back to their original sizing scheme. Ugh. At least there are free returns.

reviews.title: Update:Tread showing considerable wear after 70 miles
reviews.text:
Two star: Upper is holding up nicely. Tread is already worn out after only 70 miles. I tried ASICS because reviews talk of superior durability. So far for me upper is good, sole is not.

Update: I reached out to ASICS about the tread life. After sending pictures of tread wear after 70 miles they sent code for replacement shoe from ASICS.com.
That’s pretty awesome customer service in my book.

So far 20 miles on new Kayano fit and finish is great.

Comfort on gel-Kayano 28 is far superior to previous models.

Extra wide is a touch narrow but all in all the first 20 miles is running great.

reviews.title: Light weight and good for around town.
reviews.text: I have been using these shoes "on and off" for a couple of weeks and have found them to be just right for zipping around town, hanging out with people, and short walks in the park.  They are not hiking shoes, or court shoes.  At this price why would they be?  They are a tight slip on using two hands and are great once on.  Not for people who want slip into shoes. They breath and are NOT waterproof at all.  Easy to clean with soap, water and a brush if you smug them.  i like to wear them without socks, but that is just me.  I would order a bit larger than what you are used to.

reviews.title: These are GREAT if you have ogre feet! 🦍
reviews.text:
I bought two pairs in like-new condition via Amazon in 12 X-wide as I have all my other Skechers. They arrived in exactly new condition, and as soon as I took my first step in them I knew why they were so readily available. Though they were the right length and width for my big feet, the upper is so large that when I would take a step, it was like wearing ill-fitting flip flops and my feet came out of the shoes so far my heel was completely outside the shoe. My feet would have to be at least half-again as big around for these to fit properly. I feel like someone punked me and gave me the shoes that belonged to that green ogre from the Dreamworks movie series. These are the worst-fitting Skechers I’ve ever bought (the other bad ones have been too narrow). I went to a physical store today and bought a new pair in a different model but the same size that fits properly.

Same quality shoe, horrible fit, weird sole, returned immediately.

reviews.title: Smallish fit, 4E = 2E, and tight in the length (go a half-size up). Also, this is a SUPPORT shoe...
reviews.text: Unfortunately, they do not make this in a 2E, only the 4E. Interestingly, this is not really a true 4E, more like a 3E--as is typical of Nike products, this is narrower than advertised. I need a true 2E from Nike, and a regular D/M width in any other manufacturer (Brooks, Asics, NB). Also, these are a little short for the size. Recommend a half-size up. Note that although this is not indicated anywhere in the product listing, this is a SUPPORT shoe for people who pronate. I supinate slightly (the opposite), so this is not the right shoe for me. Solid shoes though--this is one of the most durable shoes I have ever owned--extremely durable, in fact. You will go through lots of laces before these are worn out. I wore mine for years until I finally got a proper education in supportive footwear. Turns out these are totally the wrong shoe for me and were screwing up my ankles and knees because they increased my supination (rolling of the foot outward), instead of preventing it. I need a "neutral" shoe, and these are a "support" shoe.

reviews.title: These are so comfortable. Great cushioning

Bought the grey ones in May, and bought white ones in July, and I alternate wearing them back and forth. They wash easily, just leave them out and dry in a day or two, and still look new, and it's now late February. Just curious, I looked them up on Amazon to see if they are still available for the day when I'll replace them (but not for awhile yet), and there were two left in my size for each color. So I just ordered one of each. Two shoes for $100 for excellent shoes I love, that's pretty great! I'll save these new ones until my current two are ready to replace.

reviews.title: Comfy and Stylish
reviews.text:
I ordered these sneakers in an 8.5 and an 8.5 wide. I normally wear an 8.5, sometimes a 9 and have slightly wide feet. With and without socks the 8.5 feel great. The 8.5 wide are slightly more roomy in the toe area but a little too loose when I walk. I'm leaning towards the 8.5 regular width. The footbed is nicely cushioned and the heel inside is not too high. The zippers are on both sides of each shoe and both are functional. I look forward to styling these with many outfits.

Update: I've worn them a couple of times now and I can confirm that they are pretty comfortable even after a couple hours of continuous wear. They are easy to put on and go. Only downside I am noticing is that the white sole is getting dirty pretty quickly so I will have to clean them regularly.
reviews.author: AGXAXJEWX52WIIK5J3AYQCIVEULA

reviews.title: Review from a person with difficult feet.
reviews.text: Let me preface this with the fact that I have lots of feet problems—extremely high arches, plantar fasciitis, supination etc. I do not feel i have wide feet but wides are often more comfortable so I was happy to try my normal size (8.5) in wide. The length is standard. For the price they are cute and pretty comfy overall. I do not feel the claim of memory foam provides any more comfort. The foam is so thin they feel like normal (if not slightly poorer quality) shoes. Even with the wide width I feel pressure just below the zipper on the inside of my foot. I am unsure if that will release as the shoes are worn. They are easy to get on/off and the double zippers help with my high arches. (Normally this style of shoe is really hard for me to get on because of the old arches.)  I am undecided if I will keep them yet because of the pressure near the zipper but if you have normal feet these shoes might just be acceptable for you. If you have wide feet and no other issues they may not be wide enough.

description: Ready for every day and every way. Technology for even your toughest workouts, athletic styling that you can wear on your easiest days. Imported Rubber sole

reviews.title: CURRENTLY have FOUR pairs of the Cohesion 11!
reviews.text:
EDIT: Just bought three more pairs in different colors of the Cohesion 11. They are NOT on Saucony's site any longer and the ONLY place I found them in all the colors I wanted was on Amazon from ORVA.
I am stocking up because I LOVE these shoes because they FIT my less than perfect 70 year old feet, bunions and all, and once they're gone, they're gone. No longer making them as they have moved on to revised versions.

I TRIED the Cohesion 10. Didn't like and returned. I read reviews of the 12, 13 and 14 and as the Cohesion 11's work SO well for my needs and fit perfectly, I will stay with these (and add a couple MORE pairs!)
________________________________________________________________________________________

First, I turn 70 on my next bd. I have been a Saucony fan, ONLY athletic shoe I buy, for DECADES.

These didn't disappoint.

PROS:

TOE BED - I have bunions, I broke one of my toes, so it now sticks up a bit. Not perfect feet, which is not surprising for someone turning 70. LOVE the TOE BED. Roomy, without feeling TOO roomy.

SIZE - I find these TRUE TO SIZE. Reading reviews for almost ANY shoe, you see some saying TOO BIG! some saying TOO SMALL! Well, I have worn an 8.5 in any type of athletic shoe for decades and went with that. A size smaller or larger would NOT have fit.

FABRIC - I was concerned the MESH would feel really rough. I ALWAYS wear with a short sport sock, the kind not visible, but thought the mesh might still RUB. I have had on about an hour, just walking around in the house before I decide these are a keeper and the MESH inside and out feels soft and giving, LIKE THAT VERY MUCH.

LOOKS - FIRST, I got the ALL black as I wanted to wear with my black jeans, ALL black is always dressier and doesn't stand out as a running shoe if you want something a little dressier, but also got another pair as a running shoe, little fancier re color with same fit and comfort.

PRICE - couldn't beat the price. Even lower end Saucony (and Saucony has been my brand of choice for DECADES) are not CHEAP, but got this on sale for about 39 bucks, with tax about 41. SEEM like a high quality shoe if it had cost more.

CONS:

NOT for really HARDCORE, 30 mile a week RUNNERS: These are GREAT for my purpose. Doing maybe 3 miles three or four times a week, I also rotate shoes, these aren't my only pair. The way these are designed, I don't think they would suit someone who runs five-six miles almost every day. For mild, moderate runner/joggers, I think these are a great buy.

SHOELACES: This is minor and I would still have gotten these shoes. Getting another pair of black shoelaces, not a biggie, but surprised that are SO thin and flimsy. I am not one who likes the LOOOOONG shoelaces that so many athletic shoes seem to come with, and these are a bit short, but fine. BUT FLIMSY for athletic laces.

BOTTOMLINE: FIVE STARS. Would buy these again and the price just made it better! IF YOU GET SHIPPED THRU AMAZON and ESP if you're a PRIME member like I am, you get free ship and free return ANYWAY. SO IF you've been wondering about these after reading reviews, GIVE EM A TRY, ESP if they're on sale! You can return if they don't fit.

IF I was still a runner, I would upgrade, but for my needs, doing a lot of walking, all day comfort, these are perfect and at a GREAT price.

I already have two pairs in different colors, plan on getting a couple more!

ONLY CAVEAT: laces are kind of wimpy and I'm going to replace, but that's SO MINOR, not taking a star off.

reviews.title: Coming apart &Not true to American sizes
reviews.text:
THese finally worked for me after trying Three times.
I wear an 11W normally in American sizes.
You know, the scale you put your foot on to get the size you need.
After an 11 wouldn't accept my foot - I returned them.
Ordered a 12W. That was WAY Too Tight. But long enough.
I got this size 13XW in May. It was a little tight in the width. BUT, it was as a new pair normally fits.
A little tight. Then they shape themselves to your foot. Right?
All was well until a few days ago. I stepped into a hole in the yard early morning. Between the wet grass and that hole being filled with water. My shoes are now soaking wet.
I let them dry in the sun but now they stink. I use Desonex regularly so this was surprising to me.
Into the washer they go. With some old towels and run on regular wash.
There was nothing wrong with these shoes before their bath.
These pictures are what they look like after being washed ONE Time?
The Fau-Leather is separating at the stitching. There is not a hole yet but it is Very thin now.
That is a high stress ares that flexes with each step. If these shoes can not handle One trip through the washing machine now? How do I keep buying them?
I love that they are really light. I have been wearing them for years.
But something has changed lately. The last pair made it 143 month before the sole separated and wore thru the middle. I do not have a hard step. Shoes wear normally across the entire sole.
Materials have changed. Sizing has changed.
Once you find the correct size for you they fit nicely.
Just Very Cheaply made now. Originally I would get them at Walmart. But suddenly they didn't fit and they couldn't get me the right size there.
So I looked online.
Way to ruin a good shoe Avia
I'm no longer looking at you
reviews.author: AFIDPK57IMPFMJGMERCGBWOMJTVQ

reviews.title: Selling Out Fast!
reviews.text:
I hadn’t ran in an official race for many years - so I haven’t had legit long distance running shoes since. I bought the size 7’s since I saw they were labelled as the last pair and that’s the size I’ve been wearing for the ASICS Quantum line. Once the Nimbus 22’s were delivered, I realized that I can wear these as regular shoes, but not running shoes since I need the extra space... such a shame, I can’t find 7.5’s anywhere in this same color.

Besides this, I was surprised how cushioned these felt when I first tried them on. I have wide feet and these didn’t seem too narrow.

Update:

I tried the Nimbus 22’s in both size 7 and 7.5 and they are still too small... the difference in the 7.5 is that it is wider, but not so much taller.. I was certain the half size would be enough but it doesn’t seem so, my toes barely graze the top of the shoe in the 7.5’s.

To reiterate, I wear the Quantum 360 v5’s in ***size 7*** with a thumb width’s worth at the top of the toe box. This model is taller and more narrow of a shoe than the Nimbus 22’s. It looks like I will have to try the Nimbus’s in a full size bigger than the Quantum’s, hopefully the size 8’s would be it for me.

Online shopping can be a bit frustrating but thank goodness for the return policy.

Pictured from left to right:

Quantum 360 5
GT-2000 8
Nimbus 22
reviews.author: AFXMRCGTLJ3SVDUE6QZOCHURCFCA

reviews.title: Finally back to the wider toe box
reviews.text: The Nimbus has been my go-to since 2013 and I have enjoyed all of them. The 18's went to a narrower toebox and generally narrow overall, which was fine, but did cause me some discomfort while breaking them in. The 22's feel much more like the first Nimbus's that I got years ago and I am so happy with them. I had no pain on the outside edges of my feet during my first wear, like I did with the 18's. It is interesting that these are "gender specific", as in they have a narrow scoop around the upper heel/lower ankle to keep them on the foot and supposedly something to do with the "trussic" for female gait. Not sure about that part, but my socks didn't slip down this time, and I felt great after my run/walk. I recommend these shoes. Comfy, padded and stable.

reviews.title: Why do I keep trying
reviews.text:
Skechers makes shoes I find visually appealing. They tend to be very comfy right off the bat and they make lots of models in wide sizes. However, their quality is just terrible. I bought these in June for the new school year, but ended up wearing them starting in July because my previous pair of skechers was falling apart. Now only 2 months later, the inside heal of one shoe is wearing out, the loop on the back has already snapped and the memory foam has lost its omph.

I guess I but them because the prices are cheap, but not really. They used to be cheap, but when I need to replace them in less than a year, when I used to get 2 years out of them, there's a problem and they become expensive.
I'm an adult who mainly used these to walk to work. I don't beat on them. My Mizuno running shoes have a YEAR warranty. Skechers doesn't seem to have any warranty that I can see leaving this 15+ year customer saying, don't buy them and now I need to find another shoe brand that suits me.

reviews.title: Very comfortable shoe if you don't have flat feet!
reviews.text:
The amount of comfort in this shoe is amazing. It competes easily with $130 overpronation shoes I own aside from no arch support. That's where it failed completely for me. Already tried replacing the insoles with arch support ones but the comfort level wasn't the same sadly. The provided insole is removable but is tacked down with adhesive. Didn't damage the shoes when removing it and with care I was able to put it back in.

So in conclusion, yes these are amazing shoes if you need some overpronation support or if your gait is neutral. In this case I'd highly recommend but sadly it didn't work for me. The shoes look amazing and they feel amazing. The price with the construction of the shoes is an amazing value if you just need some extra support and cushion. I'd consider this shoe to be plush in the midsole and the insole was comfortable but not a lot to make it heavy. If it weren't for my arch I would of ordered another shoe to keep in storage when this one wears out. Great service as usual from Amazon for the free return.
reviews.author: AGZVUCEB522NLEHQNXMN3ZM3UW7Q

title: New Balance Women's Fresh Foam 1080 V10 Running Shoe
description: Give your running gear a stylish update with the New Balance Fresh Foam 1080 v10. This performance running shoe delivers a sleek and impressive design that's packed with data-driven comfort. Part of the Fresh Foam X collection and made with our signature Fresh Foam cushioning, this responsive runner is made to encourage you mile after mile as you conquer short training runs, long distances and everything in between. These running shoes are our premium expression of runner’s data, supreme comfort, and cutting-edge design, all of which you'll appreciate as you lace them up ahead of a run. Experience 360-degrees of comfort in these fitness running shoes, thanks to the Ultra Heel technology and engineered knit upper. You'll love the data-driven design that features a strong and durable blown rubber outsole, a Hypoknit upper with plenty of stretch and support, and an Ortholite sockliner for underfoot comfort. Select styles of the Fresh Foam 1080 v10 may be eligible for Medicare reimbursement with Medicare/HCPCS code A5500. 50% Synthetic, 50% Mesh Imported Rubber sole Cushioned Running Shoes for Every Runner: Part of the Fresh Foam X collection, these shoes use our latest advancements in data to design to deliver precise plushness exactly where you need it Support and Durability: This shoe features a Hypoknit upper designed to provide strategic areas of stretch and support, in addition to a blown rubber outsole for impressive durability Perfect Fit: These comfortable running shoes include a bootie upper construction and an Ultra Heel design, both of which hug the back of the foot for a snug, supportive fit Breathable and Lightweight Running Shoes: The supportive engineered knit upper includes synthetic and mesh materials for amazing breathability during runs of any distance Keep Your Feet Comfortable: The Ortholite sockliner in these performance running shoes helps keep your feet feeling fresh as you power through your toughest runs

reviews.title: Tight across the top of the foot - OUCH!
reviews.text:
So on my quest for comfortable shoes with both support and cushion, I ordered these sneakers. I'm not a runner (I walk), so I was a little hesitant to spend quite this much on shoes, but I was striking out all over the place on finding shoes.

So - the good first, these shoes are REALLY cushiony (is that a word?). They feel nice and soft like walking on springy clouds. The shoes are attractive and lightweight.

The bad - the portion that is over the top of your foot, where the laces start, is so tight! I ordered a wide shoe and it was fine except for that fit, It squeezed the top of my foot terribly. I figured that they could be broken in and it would loosen up, but for that price tag, I want the shoes to fit right out of the box. From other reviews, I don't think this is due to my wide foot. If you have narrow/skinny feet or don't mind taking the time to break these in, they do have great cushioning.

title: Brooks Womens Glycerin 18 Running Shoe
description: A bit like running in the clouds. Thanks to the increased elasticity of the upper and the super-soft cushioning throughout the length, these men's running shoes are the ultimate comfort. Super soft cushioning. The revised ratio between midsole and outer sole allows you to increase LOFT DNA to achieve even more extreme softness at the sole of the foot, without losing reactivity or endurance. An OrthoLiteTM insole ensures an extraordinary wearing comfort. Imported Rubber sole THIS WOMEN'S SHOE IS FOR: The Glycerin 18 is perfect for runners who think there's no such thing as too much cushioning. The upper enhances comfort by perfectly balancing stretch and structure. SUPPORT AND CUSHION: Provides neutral support while offering the maximum amount of cushioning. Ideal for road running, cross training, the gym or wherever you might want to take them! Predecessor: Glycerin 17. SUPER-SOFT CUSHIONING: Increased DNA LOFT super-soft cushioning allows for even more extreme softness, without losing responsiveness or durability, while the OrthoLite sockliner provides premium step-in comfort. PLUSH FIT: The plush feel of an internal stretch bootie surrounds your foot and moves and expands with your stride. The engineered mesh upper enhances the fit. SMOOTH TRANSITIONS: The plush transition zone makes every move from heel to toe feel incredibly soft and smooth.

description: Free yourself from the hassle of laces. The Premix is a great-looking, classic sneaker in an easy-to-wear, slip-on style. Elastic insets ensure a snug yet comfortable fit as well as an easy on and off. Additional comfort features include a padded tongue and collar, a cushioned footbed, and an EVA midsole for great shock absorption. A durable non-marking rubber outsole delivers excellent grip. Skechers is an award-winning global leader in the lifestyle footwear industry that designs, develops, and markets lifestyle footwear that appeals to trend-savvy men, women and children. The company's success stems from it's high quality, diversified, and affordable product line that meets consumers' various lifestyle needs. Since it's inception in 1992, the Skechers diverse product offering has grown from utility style boots to include seven Skechers brands and five uniquely branded fashion lines for men and women. 100% Leather and synthetic Rubber sole Slip-on sneaker with polished hardware and cushioned tongue and collar Supportive sole Padded collar and tongue

reviews.title: Cute, easy to walk in outside. Top ankle area is tight and rigid
reviews.text:
I just bought a pair of the Sketchers knit shoes to wear around the house and I like them so much that I tried these for outdoor use. The shoe itself is not bad looking and has a reasonable amount of arch support built in.

My issue is with the top, around the top of the foot and ankle. It's just tight and inflexible. After a week of wearing these, that top is still very tight - it hasn't really broken in or loosened up. I like that these are slip on, but there still should be some give in the tongue area.

reviews.title: Highs and Lows
reviews.text: These are only $40, and being made in the USA is a plus. I am using them for inside shoes (i.e. house shoes), so I'm not expecting a lot. The soles were stiff at first but have softened up to just the right bounce after a couple of weeks (I weigh 230 lbs). I bought a 12, and was able to insert a Dr Scholl's insert without cutting it. Those insoles are cut for a 13, though it is more like a 12.5. I wear 12/13- depending upon the shoe, and these feel just right at 12. If you are a wide foot then you might feel differently, of course. The two shortcomings are the 47 in laces (too short) and the lack of protection provided by the tongue. Even with elastic laces set up to be slip-ons, there is still an intrusion of pain. It may just be me and my sensitive feet, but I don't feel this way in a pair of Hokas (though they are 3 times the price. I already wear a pretty thick cotton sock, but the laces still transmit through to my foot. I may glue so moleskin to the underside of the tongue to deflect any "point loads" away from my wimpy feet.
reviews.author: AFCP5LEBIFZXT3TNX2EPF7LP65ZQ

reviews.title: Lack of Mid-Sole Support?
reviews.text:
I purchased these after buying TWO pairs of 'Fila's that were on sale on JC Penny's
and found them 'functional but could be better" .

The reason for buying shoes from this user is for walking- and by that I mean walking about 7
miles every morning and often 3-5 miles later in the day as well.

The pair I have liked is a DOUBLE FUSION by Nike that provides soft but firm support for the entire foot
from HEAL to TOE and Mid-Sole. The Fila's just feel 'flat' and do not provide the support or cushion
this user needs since I have no cartilage left in my knees and hips. Therefore, cushioning and support both are of prime importance (I'm a former long distance runner who averaged over 100 miles/week at one point).

The Saucony I read averages 'small' so best to order 1/2 size larger than one's usual (that is what I read in comments)...so that is what I did. Must say, size wise (width/length) of this shoe in doing so, couldn't be much better. My only complaint on this end is the mid-sole feels a bit 'flat or hard' as I'd hope it would be.
I did purchase at the Dollar Store (of all places) this extra thick insole to line along the bottom of the shoe
and that made a big difference.

For added protection, the reinforced Toe Protect tip helps protect your toes from rocks, roots and debris so you can go off-road with confidence. 50% Synthetic, 50% Textile Imported Rubber sole Cushioned Running Shoes: ABZORB midsole technology in this runner absorbs impact through a combination of cushioning and compression resistance, and the rubber outsole provides strength and durability Built-In Off-Road Technology: Toe Protect technology helps protect feet from rocks, roots and debris, while AT Tread on the outsole provides versatile traction during both on- and off-road activities Plush, Cushioned Running Shoes: These runners feature lightweight injection-molded EVA foam midsoles to provide lightweight cushioning and an NB Soft + Supportive Insert for memory comfort that lasts Built to Last: With an all-over durable construction and an aggressive lugged outsole, these trail running shoes are designed to stand up to all of your outdoor activities Athletic Aesthetic: With synthetic and mesh uppers that deliver breathability and a top-quality fit, these running shoes also boast a stylish look to fit in with your active lifestyle

She has worn them twice now for short periods of time to run errands with Grandma or Grandpa & play in the yard a bit, but that's it so far. description: Dynamic adaptive fit for optimal freedom of movement, endless comfort and a supportive fit 100% Textile Imported Rubber sole Shaft measures approximately low-top from arch Adidas quality Imported by adidas

reviews.title: WAY too tight over the foot.  NOT for Diabetics, IMHO!
reviews.text:
I've had these on for about an hour now, so I am sure they are not returnable. That's too bad, because an hour has shown me that they are very uncomfortable. I cannot imagine who would find them wearable. Maybe someone with abnormally narrow feet.

The VERY stretchy material they are made from makes them hard to get on. You MUST use a shoehorn, and even then, it is not easy. They also - unpleasantly - squeeze the foot. After an hour, my toes are getting cold from low circulation! Seriously! Plus, the material does not breathe well, despite it's "perforated" appearance. So my feet are hot and wet.

The laces are just decoration. It's the tight material that holds them on. Plus, the artsy-craftsy arch inset in the instep is an invitation to soiling underneath it, without giving much arch support.
Could these be knock-offs? I assumed the attractive price was because they are a discontinued color. Maybe they are just fakes!.

PS THESE ARE NOT FOR DIABETICS. There is too much restriction of circulation.

I was advised to get "caged" Ultraboosts...the ones that lace normally. (The "cage" is the plastic framework for the laces.) But I liked the color of these.

Big mistake.

title: ASICS Men's Gel-Kahana 8 Running Shoes
description: Known for durability, stability, and its rugged style, the GEL-Kahana 8 shoe offers our GEL Cushioning System for comfort and performance where the rubber meets the road or trail. In 1949, Mr. Kihachiro Onitsuka began his athletic footwear company (Onitsuka Co., Ltd.) by manufacturing basketball shoes out of his living room in Kobe, Japan. He chose the name ASICS for his company in 1977, based on a famous Latin phrase "Anima Sana In Corpore Sano," which, when translated, expresses the ancient ideal of "A Sound Mind in a Sound Body." Taking the acronym of this phrase, ASICS was founded on the belief that the best way to create a healthy and happy lifestyle is to promote total health and fitness. Today, ASICS offers a full line of performance-driven athletic shoes and technical active sports apparel and accessories dedicated to bringing harmony to the body and soul. Rubber sole Rear foot GEL Cushioning System: Attenuates shock during impact phase and allows for a smooth transition to instance. SpEVA Midsole: Material Improves bounce-back characteristics and decreases midsole breakdown. DuoMax Support System: A dual-density midsole system positioned to enhance support and stability. Trusstic System: Reduces the weight of the sole unit while retaining the structural integrity of the shoe. Trail Specific Outsole: Reversed lugs provide uphill and downhill traction on all types of terrain. Fit Tip: If between sizes, order 0.5 size up ; Weight: 11.9 oz ; ASICS GEL-Kahana 8 is made for neutral- Foot Type:Normal size arches , Over- Foot Type :Low arches or flat feet.

reviews.title: 4th kahana.  Walk-around shoe.
reviews.text:
I don't run anymore. These are walk about town shoes. This is my 4th pair of kahanas. Go back to 5th it was trabuco.
Try kahana vs venture in a store. You'll notice the difference.
Just my walkabout pair.
Regrets?
Saucony marauder. Mizuno wave legend. NB 626.
You can fix those with scholls.
You don't have to fix these.
Lace them loose or go up a size and lace tight.
Again, not trail running in these. Simple daily about town...you won't find a better more comfortable shoe for the price. PERIOD.
If these don't do it for you go get the Mizuno wave rider. Or gt2000.
What's amusing is I have no clue when I bought my previous kahana 8s.
And I just bought another kahana 8.
Again,if you can find a better pair of walkabouts, let me know...for the price.
You won't. This is it.
Toebox has definitely narrowed from previous versions... notice the lacing. 12s still a 12.

reviews.title: Wide toe box?  NO
reviews.text: I ordered two different sizes, both wide in case the toe box was really not wide enough.  Um, so where do the toes go?!  The toe area of the shoes are pointed...they aren’t rounded or wide.  People with bunions typically have crooked toes and we need more toe room.  There is less room in the toes than almost all sneakers I’ve tried.  Anyway, if you order wide, the toe area will be narrow and the rest of the shoe sloppy, unless you truly have a wide foot.  I don’t, I just usually order wide so that my toes will fit.  I’m so disappointed!  I’ve got so much wrong with my feet and the description of these shoes made it sound like they were the answer to all of them...bunions, arches, plantar fasciitis, wide toe box.  Grrrr.

description: Add more cushioned comfort and response to your run with the Skechers Max Cushioning Elite™. A cushioned comfort running and walking design on an ULTRA GO® cushioned platform. The company's success stems from it's high quality, diversified, and affordable product line that meets consumers' Various lifestyle needs. Since it's inception in 1992, the Skechers diverse product offering has grown from utility style boots to include seven Skechers brands and five uniquely branded fashion lines for men and women. title: Rockport Men's World Tour Classic Walking Shoe Sneaker
description: The World Tour is a Rockport legend. With its relaxed fit and Rockport Walking Platform construction, the men's World Tour walking shoe delivers what it takes to go the extra mile. Additional comfort features include a padded tongue and collar. In 1971, founded on the idea that style and comfort are two sides of the same coin, Rockport was the first to introduce dress shoes infused with sport technology. Today, building on generations of shoemaking experience, Rockport continues to deliver extraordinary technology-based comfort, incorporating the latest advances in construction and design into both modern and classic dress shoes, casual shoes, boots, sandals and everywhere in between. You'll breeze through the day in comfort and style with the M7100 Pro Walker from Rockport Men's. This tried and true classic sports a textured leather upper with a rounded toe, along with padded tongue and collar for maximum comfort. Cushioned insole cradles your foot, and the lace-up design allows for a perfect fit. Rockport has always been one step ahead of the times, believing the perfect shoe has not yet been invented. This positive dissatisfaction compels them to constantly improve upon their footwear. Founded in 1971, they were the first company to use advanced athletic technologies in casual shoes to achieve lightweight comfort, and the first company to engineer walking comfort in dress and casual shoes. See more 100% Leather Imported Rubber sole Shaft measures approximately low-top from arch Full grain leather upper EVA midsole Rubber outsole Fabric K70885, K71181, K70884

reviews.title: Something happen in this last year.
reviews.text: I have been  buying Rockports for 18 years. Started out with the ProWalkers and do to the quality dimensioning I started getting World Tour Classic Walking in January 2016. Just bought the pair for this year March 1st. Something happen within the last year with quality. Like someone else said the tongue of the shoe is now too short. I haven't notice any issues with stitching my issues are my sore feet trying to break these in. I can only wear these 2 to 3 times a week before I have to switch back to my old pair. This has never happen with the Rockports and its very upsetting. I do get Platar Fasciitis so the first thing I do is put in good arch supports. So is it my arch supports that aren't doing the job which is what I thought first. When I switched back to my old pair of World Touring Classic from last year I switched the Arch support too. After a few days my feet got better. Switched back to my new pair with arch support and I am sitting here typing this dreading getting up to work because the bottom of my feet hurt.

reviews.title: Runs Small
reviews.text:
These are great sneakers if you choose the right size. The exterior fabric is lightweight and breathable. The mesh allows for good air flow. This also makes the fabric quicker to dry if it gets wet. The insoles have good arch support and great overall cushioning. The soles have excellent traction on any surface. The sizing runs a few centimeters short of standard US sizing. If you choose your normal size, your toes may be a little cramped for space. If you prefer a little breathing room, size up. These shoes are good for all types of exercise and for streetwear. I highly recommend this product.

Thanks so much for taking the time to read this review. I hope the information that I've provided makes your decision a little easier.

reviews.title: Best in Class

Adidas makes AMAZING running shoes. They're light as a feather and respond to your momentum. This pair also looks very stylish. reviews.title: Crazy expensive for Made in Vietnam, but I like them.
reviews.text:
These shoes have a weight of 12oz each. Not super heavy, but not super lightweight either. Though you can see a thick white foam on the sole. . .The thickness of the bottom of the shoe is only about an 1/8 of an inch. If you’re running on a track or treadmill, that will probably not be an issue. But if you’re running on the road, I doubt the tread is going to last too long.

Padding on the inside is super-firm. Nice arch support. Nice firm padding around the heel / ankle.

Mesh breathable upper. The upper is all one piece, and the tongue is not separate. As you can from the pictures. . . .The laces are placed through plastic “shields” on each side of the shoe.

These shoes are Made in Vietnam. They are comfortable, and there is extra “bounce”, but I can’t say that they are worth the crazy expensive price. Nonetheless, I really like them.
reviews.author: AG375WAXLZ7PIOQKIQ6KQB4J3JVQ

reviews.title: So comfortable... like walking on air!
reviews.text:
The Adidas men’s Ultraboost 21’s are amaaaazing quality sneakers. My son wanted them, and I myself was not too sure about the pink on the bottom, however my teenager doesn’t mind the color at all. He actually loves the color combination.

The fit is perfect, true to size... and they are SO comfortable... almost like walking on air! You get that little bounce when you walk, and even more when running. They are well cushioned to keep your feet happy. They are a bit pricey, but you get what you pay for and these are worth it!

reviews.title: Best shoes for neuropathy pain EVER!!
reviews.text: I suffer from peripheral neuropathy which causes pain in my feet. I need to run to get my exercise but, because of the pain it can turn a 10 mile run into a 5 mile run. These shoes are the absolute best I have ever worn. I mean, I love ASICS but, these are by far the best ASICS I have owned so far. The cushioning you feel with each step goes a long way in making sure I finish my daily 10 mile run. I highly recommend these to my fellow diabetics who suffer from peripheral neuropathy. Your feet will thank you.

reviews.title: Umpteenth pair of these shoes.  Work great for my feet
reviews.text: I've been running in ASICS Nimbuses for well over a decade now.  They just work great for my feet, provide good durability for my mileage needs, and keep me running comfortably and injury free, provided I replace them at normal intervals.  I recommend them to anyone who needs a cushion shoe.  Before biting the bullet though, if you're an inexperienced runner, figure out what you need, whether that be cushioning, stability, etc., what works with your arches, footstrike, etc.

reviews.title: Pleasantly surprised!
reviews.text:
I wasn’t expecting these to work, I usually get Innov8’s but they are >$100 and stopped carrying my women’s size 12, so I was searching for another option. I got Mens 11 and even though I have extremely narrow women’s feet, these work very well. The toe box is wider than it needs to be on me, but the laces and strict of the shoe keeps my feet stable. I actually like the connected tongue as well.

I use these mainly for trail walking (5-10 miles a week) and gym workouts.

To sum it up, these are great quality and performance barefoot style shoes that are a surprisingly good price! I love how my foot feels connected with the ground but still has enough cushion to make it comfortable.
reviews.author: AHIXGREYZQKKBNWWXCZJUBUUKRUA

reviews.title: Amazing!
reviews.text:
I won't use any other shoe for working out from now on. Most workout shoes I've tried were cushioned nicely but with a thick, high sole my feet tended to bend in weird ways. These are by far the most comfortable and practical shoe I've ever worn, and I like being closer to the ground. They've held up pretty well so far but I haven't done a whole lot of hiking in them yet, mostly gym use. What hiking I have done has been great, very comfortable, and no issues.

They're a great price as well for someone like me who is pretty hard on shoes and goes trough them fairly often, usually a pair every 1-2 years when it's workout/hiking shoes.

title: ASICS Gel-Excite 4 Men's Running Shoe
description: For moderate mileage runners, the GEL-Excite 4 shoe offers comfort and durability. Our signature Rearfoot and forefoot gel cushioning technology softens brisk impact, while our ahar outsole targets high-impact zones for increased resistance wear and tear. In 1949, Mr. Kihachiro Onitsuka began his athletic footwear company (Onitsuka Co., Ltd.) by manufacturing basketball shoes out of his living room in Kobe, Japan. He chose the name ASICS for his company in 1977, based on a famous Latin phrase "Anima Sana In Corpore Sano," which, when translated, expresses the ancient ideal of "A Sound Mind in a Sound Body." Taking the acronym of this phrase, ASICS was founded on the belief that the best way to create a healthy and happy lifestyle is to promote total health and fitness. Today, ASICS offers a full line of performance-driven athletic shoes and technical active sports apparel and accessories dedicated to bringing harmony to the body and soul. 100% Synthetic Imported Rubber sole Rearfoot GEL Cushioning System: Attenuates shock during impact phase and allows for a smooth transition to midstance. Removable Sockliner: A sockliner which can be removed to accommodate a medical orthotic. AHAR Outsole: Acronym for ASICS High Abrasion Rubber. Placed in critical areas of the outsole for exceptional durability. Reflectivity

In 1949, Mr. Kihachiro Onitsuka began his athletic footwear company (Onitsuka Co., Ltd.) by manufacturing basketball shoes out of his living room in Kobe, Japan. He chose the name ASICS for his company in 1977, based on a famous Latin phrase "Anima Sana In Corpore Sano," which, when translated, expresses the ancient ideal of "A Sound Mind in a Sound Body." Taking the acronym of this phrase, ASICS was founded on the belief that the best way to create a healthy and happy lifestyle is to promote total health and fitness. title: ASICS Gel-Nimbus 20 Women's Running Shoe
description: The Gel-Nimbus 20 shoe has a flexible vertical groove that decouples tools along the progression line to improve gait efficiency. The exoskeletal heel counterweight provides improved support and creates an improved heel fitment environment. It has a gel cushioning system for rear hind and forefoot, that reduces the impact during the detachment phases and allows the movement on several levels while the foot passes through the step cycle.The material of the speva midsole improves the characteristics of rebound and reduces midsole breakage. It has a new blown ahar outsole that offers advanced cushioning, durability and foot stroke, in addition it is built with the innovative midsole foam, flytefoam. Its organic fibers bounce metarun provide a perfect cushioning from the beginning of the stroke to the end.It is designed with a 3 m reflective material, for visibility. Textile and Synthetic Imported Rubber sole Shaft measures approximately low-top from arch FLYTEFOAM MIDSOLE TECHNOLOGY Our FlyteFoam technology provides exceptional bounce back and responsiveness no matter the distance, utilizing organic super fibers to help reduce packing out that traditionally happens with softer, low density foams. FLUIDRIDE MIDSOLE: ASICS FluidRide midsole provides the ultimate combination of bounce back and cushioning properties with reduced weight and exceptional durability.

reviews.title: I won't buy these anymore.
reviews.text: I have been wearing Asics Gel Nimbus 20s for quite a few years now; I think I got my first pair 5-7 years ago but I'm not sure. At any rate, the pair I bought last month will be my last pair, because these shoes are not what they once were. The company seems to have chosen cost savings over comfort. They used to be like stepping onto a cloud, but I think I would have done better to invest in a pair of Super Feet insoles for my old ones instead. These new gels are not as cushy or supportive as in the past. I'm sure the company went with cheaper materials. Next time, I will go with a new company.
reviews.author: AEC5IZ5JMOSOUXT5NDVAYV6JTL7Q

reviews.title: Great Fit
reviews.text: This is my favorite running/walking shoe. Asics are not making the Gel Nimbus in narrows any more and  I was so glad to see this one on Amazon. Thank YOU!. This shoe is great for narrow feet. Hugs your foot with support, but just enough room in the toe box for expansion. I've always had to size up in tennis shoe. I normally wear an 8N. The 9 AA in the Gel Nimbus 20 is perfect for my foot.

reviews.title: A comfortable shoe for casual use
reviews.text: [[VIDEOID:1015be58bd6250fd72bf29d4d5aef0af]] This shoe is very comfortable and the stitching and construction are very good.  I like how my feet feel snug in this shoe.  The high back heel support and high shoe tongue helps to cradle my foot.  I really like this shoe.  However, I do Not see this shoe as a replacement for an athletic sneaker.  The material used on the sole is all foam.  It will not withstand high athletic use.  For example, pavement basketball and running will tear up these bottoms really quick.  I really wish that they would have made this shoe with a rubber bottom.  The non-slip sole description is limited at best.  Nonetheless, I like this shoe and use it for casual purposes and work.

reviews.title: My Favorite Pair of Shoes!!!
reviews.text: I just ordered my second pair after having my first pair for over 2 years. I loved them so much that while telling someone how comfortable they were, they happened to mention I should order another pair. Brilliant! So....I did!!  Initially I didn't want to use this pair to go to the park, walk dogs or on a messy rain day. But...they are so comfortable that eventually you just forget to change shoes. They become worn and older looking. So now, I have my Yard Pair for everyday yard work, dog walks and just around the house and errands. And I also have a nice pair I can keep clean and neat. They stretch in all the right places without binding. Great for people with bunions!
reviews.author: AGIJFWFE723DPIAOSBNRH67PGEUQ
